hypothesis: Incorporating ternary metal sublattices (two distinct metal species) into the chemical template framework will yield metal superhydrides with non-integer stoichiometries exhibiting superconducting transition temperatures exceeding 200 K at moderate pressures below 150 GPa, because the electronic heterogeneity introduced by mixed-metal quasi-atom sites will simultaneously enhance hydrogen cage distortion, increase the density of states at the Fermi level, and strengthen electron-phonon coupling beyond what is achievable in any binary metal superhydride discovered through the current binary-only template screening workflow.
